links.evidence: |year=2018 |title=Ploceus cucullatus |volume=2018 |article-number=e.T22718921A132122615 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2018-2.RLTS.T22718921A132122615.en |access-date=21 August 2021}} | genus = Ploceus | species = cucullatus | authority = (Müller, 1776) | synonyms = *Oriolus cucullatus Müller, 1776 | synonyms_ref = | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text | range_map = Ploceus cucullatus map.png | range_map_caption = }} The village weaver (Ploceus cucullatus), also known as the spotted-backed weaver or black-headed weaver (the latter leading to easy confusion with P. melanocephalus), is a bird species in the family Ploceidae found in much of sub-Saharan Africa

links.evidence: |year=2018 |title=Ploceus cucullatus |volume=2018 |article-number=e.T22718921A132122615 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2018-2.RLTS.T22718921A132122615.en |access-date=21 August 2021}} | genus = Ploceus | species = cucullatus | authority = (Müller, 1776) | synonyms = *Oriolus cucullatus Müller, 1776 | synonyms_ref = | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text | range_map = Ploceus cucullatus map.png | range_map_caption = }} The village weaver (Ploceus cucullatus), also known as the spotted-backed weaver or black-headed weaver (the latter leading to easy confusion with P. melanocephalus), is a bird species in the family Ploceidae found in much of sub-Saharan Africa

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It has also been introduced to Portugal and Venezuela as well as to the islands of Hispaniola, Martinique, Puerto Rico, Mauritius and Réunion. This often abundant species occurs in a wide range of open or semi-open habitats, including woodlands and human habitation, and frequently forms large noisy colonies in towns, villages, and hotel grounds
